titleStmt.title: Letter from Royal College of Physicians of Ireland to Dr. Maurice Hennigan O'Sullivan, 27 January 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Typescript letter requesting Dr Sullivan to sends the required statutory declaration of identification, fee and his diplomas, and his request will be heard at the next meeting of RCPI. (See related letters RCPI/2/3/2/13/153 and RCPI/2/3/1/58/T58)

msIdentifier.repository: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

correspAction.persName: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland
correspAction.placeName: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland, Dublin

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
RCPI/2/3/2/12/154 27th January
Dr. M.H. O’Sullivan. Bantry Co. Cork.
Dear Sir,
In reply to your letter of the 26th inst., I beg to ask you kindly to forward the following:
1. A statutory declaration of identification.
2.A P.O. for the sum of £1:1/-. It would be as well to forward at the same time the diplomas
which you hold from the college so that they may be endorsed
with the change of name and initialled by the Registrar. The college will meet on Friday, the 4th February and
your application will be considered at that meeting. Kindly
address the application to the Registrar. Royal College of
Physicians of Ireland, 6 Kildare Street. I remain,
Yours faithfully.
